summary: The USMCA review dominates the day. Washington's new 50% tariff on Canadian imports under Section 338 adds strain to trilateral talks that Sheinbaum pushed forward with Trump and Carney at the World Cup final, as a fresh negotiating round opened in Mexico City. Citi cited that same uncertainty, plus expected Banxico moves, in forecasting the peso weakening toward 17.92 per dollar by year-end. Separately, Nu began operating as a bank in Mexico, intensifying competition as Banxico and CNBV manage fintech-to-bank conversion risks.

lead.h1: US imposes 50% tariff on Canadian imports, straining North American trade pact
lead.context: The tariff, applied under Section 338 of the 1930 Tariff Act, hits Canadian goods even when they meet USMCA content rules, adding pressure to the trilateral review Mexico is also negotiating.
lead.background: Section 338 of the 1930 Tariff Act lets Washington impose duties even on goods that meet a trade agreement's content rules, and about 85% of Mexico's exports currently enter the US tariff-free under USMCA.
lead.drivers: Washington has escalated tariff actions against Canada even where USMCA content rules are met, adding to existing Section 232 duties on steel, aluminum and automotive goods.
lead.implications: The move sets a precedent that could be applied to Mexico's own tariff-free access under the same agreement.

coverage.title: US imposes 50% tariff on Canadian imports, straining North American trade pact
coverage.summary: The tariff, applied under Section 338 of the 1930 Tariff Act, hits Canadian goods even when they meet USMCA content rules, adding pressure to the trilateral review Mexico is also negotiating.

items.headline: Citi survey sees peso weakening toward 17.92 per dollar by year-end
items.context: The forecast ties the currency's expected slide to uncertainty over the USMCA review and anticipated Banxico moves in the second half of the year.
items.background: Banxico targets 3% inflation and sets its policy rate partly to keep Mexican rates above US levels, a differential that supports the peso and that investors watch closely.
items.drivers: Citi's July survey points to depreciation as USMCA negotiations create uncertainty and analysts anticipate shifts in the Banxico-Fed rate gap.
items.implications: The consensus implies the peso would give back part of its 2.86% first-half gain, closing 2026 near 17.92 pesos per dollar versus 17.49 at midyear.

coverage.title: Citi survey sees peso weakening toward 17.92 per dollar by year-end
coverage.summary: The forecast ties the currency's expected slide to uncertainty over the USMCA review and anticipated Banxico moves in the second half of the year.

items.headline: Nu begins operating as a bank in Mexico as Banorte welcomes competition
items.context: The Brazilian fintech's move into full banking status intensifies competition in Mexico's banking sector, according to Banorte executives.
items.background: Nu Mexico joins fintechs such as Plata and Revolut that already hold full banking licenses, while Mercado Pago, Konfío and Bunq are still awaiting approval.
items.drivers: Banorte's CEO said the bank backs free competition as long as new entrants operate under the same regulation as incumbents.
items.next: Mercado Pago, Konfío, Bunq and other fintechs remain in the queue for banking-license approval.

coverage.title: Nu begins operating as a bank in Mexico as Banorte welcomes competition
coverage.summary: The Brazilian fintech's move into full banking status intensifies competition in Mexico's banking sector, according to Banorte executives.

items.headline: Banxico and CNBV work to manage risks as fintechs convert into banks
items.context: Regulators are responding as fintech firms obtain banking licenses in Mexico, intensifying competition with incumbent lenders.
items.background: A fintech seeking a full Mexican banking license needs approval from the CNBV, the finance ministry and Banxico, and more fintechs are now pursuing that status.
items.drivers: Fintech association president Felipe Vallejo called it fintech's third transformation into banks, pushing incumbent banks to move faster while regulators weigh how to manage the added technological and remote-banking risk.

coverage.title: Banxico and CNBV work to manage risks as fintechs convert into banks
coverage.summary: Regulators are responding as fintech firms obtain banking licenses in Mexico, intensifying competition with incumbent lenders.

items.headline: Sheinbaum presses USMCA talks with Trump and Carney at World Cup final
items.context: The brief in-person exchange coincided with the opening of a new round of trilateral trade talks in Mexico City on the treaty's review.
items.background: USMCA links Mexico, the US and Canada, and after the first joint review on 1 July 2026 the US declined a 16-year extension, so the pact now faces annual reviews under Article 34.7 through 2036.
items.drivers: Washington's new 50% tariff on Canadian goods, applied even to treaty-compliant products, is pushing Mexico to seek elimination of duties on its own rules-of-origin-compliant exports.
items.implications: The Canadian tariff, applied despite compliance with the treaty, shows Washington willing to tax partner goods even under USMCA rules.
items.next: Sheinbaum was preparing to host USTR Jamieson Greer in Mexico City as the new round of talks opened.

coverage.title: Sheinbaum presses USMCA talks with Trump and Carney at World Cup final
coverage.summary: The brief in-person exchange coincided with the opening of a new round of trilateral trade talks in Mexico City on the treaty's review.

standing.text: The peso trades at 17.40 pesos to the dollar; inflation is 3.37%; the policy rate is 6.50%.
